programmes.show_description: The Strain: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan decides to do some weight-training... and uses his girlfriend Doreen as the weight.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Crazy for You: Detective drama series. When a psychiatrist's wife is murdered in a frenzied attack, Wycliffe finds that proving a motive is harder than catching the killer.
Old Habits: Detective series. Interest is reawakened in unresolved serial killings when pathologist Franks notices similarities with a young woman found strangled.
The Gift of Promise: When a businesswoman is bludgeoned to death, it looks like a case of blackmail gone wrong but there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case.
The Murder at the Vicarage: Colonel Protheroe is intensely disliked, but when he is found slumped over the desk in a vicarage study, Miss Marple must unpick the truth.
The Italian Rapscallion: Drama series about sleuthing gardeners. A mysterious death occurs while Rosemary and Laura are preparing the garden of a restaurant that is opening in Italy.
Swords into Ploughshares: Light-hearted series about a pair of sleuthing horticulturalists. An archaeologist who posed as Rosemary to gain access to an estate is found dead.
Hidden Depths: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a solicitor falls from a roof to his death. A revenge motive emerges when the dead man's rival also dies - but who is behind it?
Sauce for the Goose: Barnaby and Scott delve into the murder of a visitor to a relish factory and discover a web of secrets and lies within the family that own it.
Midsomer Rhapsody: Barnaby and Scott investigate a web of family feuds and long-lost love affairs when an inspirational retired music teacher is battered to death with a torch.
The House in the Woods: Barnaby and acting DC Ben Jones investigate the garrotting of a husband and wife outside a deserted cottage.
Parent Not Expected: DCI Vera Stanhope must unravel a tangled web of deceit between two wildly different families when the body of a teenage boy is discovered near a salmon farm.
Crazy for You: Detective drama series. When a psychiatrist's wife is murdered in a frenzied attack, Wycliffe finds that proving a motive is harder than catching the killer.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Strangers: Detective drama series. Wycliffe and the team are called in to investigate the tragic events that unfold after a wedding with a bizarre guest list.
Close to Home: Detective drama series. Wycliffe and the team are baffled when a known petty criminal turns into a killer, and Kelsey receives some bad news.
Generation of Vipers: Lewis and Hathaway are drawn into a world of virtual bullying - but very real murder - when a professor is found dead after a dating video is leaked online.
Game: Crime drama. Throwing himself into work to mask the heartache of Joan leaving, a young Endeavour Morse is quickly consumed by his duties when a research scientist is drowned.
Uneasy Lies the Head: Comedy-drama series. Martin struggles to deal with Louisa's news while trying to treat a headmaster with a strange affliction that affects one of his arms.
Up the Garden Path: The organiser of a Garden Open Day Scheme calls on Rosemary and Laura to save the good name of her village when she discovers that its gardens are blighted.
...They Ask Evans? Part one of a three-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1934 novel. Ex-naval lieutenant Bobby Jones drifts idly through life until he finds a dying man...
...They Ask Evans? The second part of a three-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's novel. The death of Bobby's friend and the local police's obduracy hardens Bobby's resolve.
...They Ask Evans? The concluding part of a three-part adaptation of Agatha Christie's 1934 novel. Frankie and Bobby approach the heart of a dark conspiracy - and an answer.
4:50 from Paddington: When Elspeth McGillicuddy sees a woman on a train being strangled, the authorities think that she has been dreaming, but Miss Marple knows better...
Dead Letters: Midsomer Barton is celebrating Oak Apple Week when a mother is found drowned - it looks like suicide, but Barnaby investigates and is shaken by memories of his past.
Dirty: The murder of a cleaner whose death occurred several hours after the killer blow landed puts DCI Vera Stanhope on an intriguing trail while piecing his final hours together.
Captivating mystery thriller. Jo and Sam interrogate Donna - who yields a revelation that points fingers at the Benson family again - before a surprise confession rocks the case.
